reviews.review_msg: I found this course to be one of the most fun I had at SFU even though it was online. I guess policy isn't a lot about theory which made the topics interesting to us. There was a lot of variety with the guest lectures as well.

I loved this course but I can't believe I got an A- i because I found the material challenging. Prof Ali was very good with communication and the course is very professionally run. There is a lot of material for an online course and it's not a bird course by any means so approach with caution.

This class was great I learned so so much. The exams were kinda hard but I found the course super rewarding. The prof is super caring and responsive over email as are the TAs. The content from all sorts of policy profs were great too. I thought the course was well organised and I was able to pay attention the whole time

Interesting and a class I enjoyed learning in. BUT, for an online class/ a first year class, I found the midterm and final to be extraordinarily difficult on the mcq. Reading specific and tricky on purpose.

This class was very reading specific and tough, to be frank I wouldn't take it as a gpa booster, but the prof and the TA's were lovely and responded fast and with pretty helpful information. The midterm and final were difficult since it was so reading specific, the assignments were very easy and were graded lightly as well!
honestly a great class. lots of reading but learned a lot.
only 2 things I have to say is I find the course content interesting, but the wording on some of the quizzes makes my cortisol levels rise.

A loooooot of reading!!!!! even more reading than you'd find in a poli sci course. The readings aren't difficult to understand, but it is a lot to digest.. especially since tests and quizzes can ask you to remember specific phrases verbatim

It's taking a while for marks to be returned, so i cant rlly critique marking atm

Solid lecture videos that are divided into digestible chunks.

30-50 pages of reading per week.

Tests are meant to test your "memory retention, understanding core concepts, critical thinking skills, and critical reading skills".

You have to be familiar with content covered in readings and lectures to do well on tests.
